steps.title: Find the slope of line γ
steps.content: Perpendicular lines have slopes that are negative reciprocals. Line $k$ has slope $m$, so line $\gamma$ has slope $-\dfrac{1}{m}$.

steps.title: Move from the intersection point to x = 8
steps.content: Line $\gamma$ passes through $(7,5)$. Moving $\Delta x=1$ to $x=8$: $$\Delta y = -\frac{1}{m}\cdot 1=-\frac{1}{m}$$ So the point is $\left(8,\,5-\dfrac{1}{m}\right)$.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• A: $\left(8,5-\dfrac{1}{m}\right)$ — matches our result. That matches choice A — A is correct.
  • B: $\left(8,5+\dfrac{1}{m}\right)$ — wrong sign; perpendicular slope is negative reciprocal, not positive. Incorrect.
  • C: $(8,5-m)$ — uses $m$ itself rather than $\tfrac{1}{m}$. Incorrect.
  • D: $(8,5+m)$ — uses $+m$; both the sign and the value are wrong. Incorrect.
